title: ds-viz-singlevalueradial - option matrix
description: Test bench for splunk.singlevalueradial. Each panel exercises one option group: maxValue (the fill ceiling), majorValue / majorColor, radialBackgroundColor (track) vs. radialStrokeColor (filled arc), trendDisplay (absolute / percent / off), shouldAbbreviateTrendValue + thousand separators, dynamic colour via DOS / rangeValue, backgroundColor override. SPL is makeresults-based.

options.query: | makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval pct = round(92 + sin(i/4)*3 + random()%4) | table _time pct
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval pct = round(58 + sin(i/4)*5 + random()%4) | table _time pct
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval pct = round(28 + sin(i/4)*4 + random()%4) | table _time pct
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval used_gb = round(420 + i*8 + random()%20) | table _time used_gb
| makeresults count=24 | streamstats count as i | eval _time = relative_time(now(), "-24h") + (i*3600) | eval orders = round(420 + i*15 + random()%30) | table _time orders

options.markdown:
# `splunk.singlevalueradial` - option matrix

A single value rendered as a radial fill (a ring). The 10.4 reference exposes 16 options - the singlevalue value+trend+chrome groups MINUS sparklines / icons / font-size locks, PLUS three radial-specific options: `maxValue` (the fill ceiling, default 100), `radialBackgroundColor` (the track), `radialStrokeColor` (the filled arc, defaults to `majorColor`). Use it for *percentage of a known whole* — quotas, capacity, completion. **Only renders inside Absolute layout.**

viz_default.title: 1. Default - high pct (92), maxValue=100
viz_default.description: Pure default. majorValue is the last point, maxValue defaults to 100 so the value is also the percent fill. radialStrokeColor falls back to majorColor (theme primary). Reads as 'almost full'.

viz_mid_pct.title: 2. Mid pct (62) - showing track + arc
viz_mid_pct.description: Same options, mid value. Makes the contrast between `radialBackgroundColor` (the unfilled track) and `radialStrokeColor` (the filled arc) visible. The track is theme-default; only the arc is the message.

viz_low_pct.title: 3. Low pct (32) - inverse-readable
viz_low_pct.description: Low value. The eye reads the *unfilled* portion as 'how much is left'. For metrics where low is bad (uptime, completion), pair with dynamic majorColor.

viz_custom_max.title: 4. maxValue 1000 - non-percentage radial
viz_custom_max.description: maxValue = 1000 lets the radial display absolute progress against a target (orders towards a daily quota of 1000). The number stays as-is; only the fill ratio changes.

viz_dynamic_arc.title: 5. Dynamic radialStrokeColor via rangeValue
viz_dynamic_arc.description: radialStrokeColor uses Dynamic Options Syntax to traffic-light the arc. Pattern: `> primary | seriesByName(field) | lastPoint() | rangeValue(thresholds)`. Arc colour follows the value; majorColor stays neutral so the number doesn't compete.

viz_dynamic_major.title: 6. Dynamic majorColor (number colour, fixed arc)
viz_dynamic_major.description: Inverse pattern to #5: arc stays a calm hue, number flips on threshold. Reads as 'this value is in trouble' without redrawing the arc geometry.

viz_radial_bg.title: 7. Custom radialBackgroundColor (track)
viz_radial_bg.description: radialBackgroundColor sets the *unfilled* track. Default is a low-alpha theme tone. Override when the theme background is unusual or you want the track to disappear (set to transparent or to the panel bg).

viz_no_trend.title: 8. trendDisplay: off (static radial)
viz_no_trend.description: Single-row data source, no time. trendDisplay = 'off' suppresses the delta. The radial alone carries the message - common pattern for quotas, completion bars, and configuration health.

viz_disk_dynamic.title: 9. Disk usage - both arc + number flip
viz_disk_dynamic.description: Combines #5 and #6. Both `radialStrokeColor` AND `majorColor` are DOS-driven against the same thresholds. maxValue = 1000 (GB). Use sparingly - it's a strong visual but multiple of these compete on the same dashboard.

viz_dynamic_bg.title: 10. Dynamic backgroundColor (whole tile)
viz_dynamic_bg.description: backgroundColor flips the entire tile on threshold. Lock majorColor + radialStrokeColor to a contrast colour so the radial stays readable when the bg goes dark. Use sparingly - one alarm tile per dashboard.

viz_separators_off.title: 11. shouldUseThousandSeparators - false
viz_separators_off.description: Strips commas. Less common for radials than for plain singlevalue (radials usually display percentages or small absolute numbers), but works the same way.

viz_precision.title: 12. numberPrecision 2 + unitPosition before
viz_precision.description: numberPrecision = 2 gives two decimals. unitPosition = 'before' is the standard for currency. Pattern for 'X.XX$ of Y$' style metrics rendered as a radial.
